title: Immalia

{{CelestialBody
|image=[[File:Immalia-EGTW.png]]
|name=Immalia
|region=*[[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]<ref name="Location">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the [[Immalia system/Legends|Immalia system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square K-8 as part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Immalia sector/Legends|Immalia sector]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' designates the Expansion Region section of that grid square as part of the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]]. In addition, {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-expansion-region|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Expansion Region}} places the Immalia [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the portion of the Expansion Region between the [[Mondress sector/Legends|Mondress]] and [[Lostar sector/Legends|Lostar sectors]]. ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' establishes both Mondress and Lostar sectors to be a part of the [[Outer Expansion Zone/Legends|Outer Expansion Zone]], which the [[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]] further defines as a sub-region located in the northern section of the Expansion Region. Therefore, Immalia must also be a part of the Outer Expansion Zone.</ref>

{{Quote|Bearing an experimental shield generator and a shimmering new energy skin, the cannonship ''Squintpipe'' threaded the enemy formation at Immalia, halting the orbital bombardment and sending the surviving ships scurrying for the jump beacon.|Supreme Chancellor Nagratha|<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-4-the-rise-of-the-republic-needs-author-entry|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 4: The Rise of the Republic}}</ref>}}
'''Immalia''' was an [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] celestial body located in the [[Immalia sector/Legends|Immalia sector]], [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] known as [[Immalian]]s. During the [[Waymancy Storm]], the [[Signatories of Waymancy]] carried out an [[Bombardment of Immalia|orbital bombardment]] of Immalia, which was halted by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] via the use of the experimental [[cannonship]] ''[[Squintpipe]]''.

[[Millennium/Legends|Millennia]] later, the astronomical object was the site of [[Fighting on Immalia|fighting]] between [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces. When the Alliance force noticed an Imperial [[Squad/Legends|squad]] donning [[radiation zone assault trooper armor]], the Rebels incorrectly assumed they were fighting in a [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] area and retreated.

==Description==
Immalia was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="IH" /> located in the [[Immalia system/Legends|Immalia system]]. It was a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Immalia sector/Legends|Immalia sector]], being located both in the [[Outer Expansion Zone/Legends|Outer Expansion Zone]] sub-region and the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] portion of that region.<ref name="Location" />

==History==
===Republic warfare===
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Waymancy Storm]]<ref name="Author's Cut" /> of [[7811 BBY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> a [[Hyperspace beacon/Legends|hyperspace jump beacon]] was situated in the vicinity of Immalia. During that conflict, the [[Signatories of Waymancy]] carried out an [[Bombardment of Immalia|orbital bombardment]] of the celestial body. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[cannonship]] ''[[Squintpipe]]'', equipped with an experimental [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]] and an [[energy skin]], threaded the formation of the Signatories' [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. As a result, the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] was ended and the surviving vessels of the Republic's enemy rushed to the jump beacon to escape the [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. At a speech delivered on the [[25th anniversary of Victory in the Waymancy Storm]] in [[7786 BBY]], the Republic's [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Nagratha]] noted the Republic action at Immalia as one of the heroic moments of the Waymancy Storm.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

When the first campaigns of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] were waged between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Fifteenth Army]] of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]], based in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]], was tasked with defending the territory known as the [[Immalia Oversector]] against the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the Confederacy.<ref name="EGTW" />

===Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|During the fighting on Immalia, we retreated when a squad of approaching radtroopers was spotted. We assumed we were operating in a contaminated zone.|General Carlist Rieekan, in a margin note in a copy of ''Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide''|<ref name="IH" />}}
At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was fought between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]<ref name="IH" /> from [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Immalia was the site of [[Fighting on Immalia|fighting]] between Imperial and Rebel forces, the latter of which included Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]]. During the confrontation, the Empire outfitted a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with [[radiation zone assault trooper armor]]. When the Rebel force spotted the approaching squad, it assumed the presence of [[Radiation zone assault trooper|radtroopers]] meant the combat was occurring in an area that had been contaminated by [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] and subsequently retreated.<ref name="IH" />

Rieekan later learned the true circumstances of the incident on Immalia, which<ref name="IH" /> he<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|generalcarlistrieekan|Rieekan, General Carlist}}</ref> recorded in a margin note in a captured copy of the an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] manual ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''.<ref name="IH" /> Immalia later fell within the borders of the Rebel Alliance's successor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Immalian}}
{{Quote|The Immalians had a small outpost there to protect their mining operations from pirate attacks.|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], on Mayvitch 7|<ref name="AoW1">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 1|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 1]]</ref>}}
Immalia was home to<ref name="Planetnamia" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="AoW1" /> population known as the [[Immalian]]s. They attempted to [[Colonist/Legends|colonize]] [[Mayvitch 7]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the<ref name="CSWE" /> nearby<ref name="EGTW" /> [[Chalenor system]],<ref name="CSWE" /> establishing [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations there<ref name="AoW1" /> by [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]<ref name="33BBY">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 1|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 1]] is set thirty-three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> as well as a small outpost for defense against [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] attacks. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the warlike [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] launched an [[Battle of Mayvitch 7|attack]] on the Mayvitch 7 colony,<ref name="AoW1" /> [[Death/Legends|killing]] most of its population.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The name "Immalia" was used to refer to the Immalia [[Star/Legends|star]] system in the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', authored by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]];<ref name="TEA" /> the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to that book;<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> and the [[2012]] book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by Fry and [[Paul R. Urquhart]].<ref name="EGTW" /> ''The Essential Atlas'' placed the system in grid square K-8.<ref name="TEA" />

A reference to Immalia as a distinct astronomical object was originally meant to be included in a section authored by Wallace for ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''; however, the section was cut before the book's release. The information regarding Immalia was later released in [[2014]] as part of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], a series of articles published on the StarWars.com [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]] by Fry and<ref name="Author's Cut" /> [[Del Rey]] editor<ref name="Celestials">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> [[Erich Schoeneweiss]] containing various cut content from ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''.<ref name="Author's Cut" />
